ety: S. ज+गातु, by redupl.; cf. P. jā, and gāh, and jāʼe-gāh
def: s.f. Place, station, quarter, locality; room, stead; vacant space, blank; opening, vacancy, post, appointment, situation; fit occasion (as for anger, &c.)
more: n. f. 1. Place; point; quarter ( asthān , 1, 2.). 1. Khālī jagah dekh-kar hāth mārā . The unguarded place is always attacked. 2. Tum apnī jagah, maiṅ apnī jagah . Keep your own place, and I will keep mine. 3. Patthar apnī jagah bhārī hai . In its own bed every stone is heavy. (Every cock crows on his own dunghill). 2. Room; stead; place. Chalnā hai, rahnā nahīṅ, mohe andesā aur, Jagah banāī hai nahīṅ, baiṭhoge kis ṭhaur? Depart I must, I cannot stay, I'm full of anxious care, No place, my soul, have you prepared, what seat can you have there? 3. Post; office; appointment; situation; a vacancy. 4. Occasion; time. Khafgī kī jagah nahīṅ hai. jagah jagah , adv. Every where. jagah chhoṛnā , v. a. 1. To leave any place. 2. To leave a blank. jagah denā , v. n. To place; make room for; lodge. jagah sir yā se honā , v. n. To be in its proper place; to be opportune or proper. jagah-gher . A. imtan…
subs: v.n. To leave a place; to leave a vacant space or blank
(-ko), To give place or room (to); to place; to make room (for), to lodge
jagah-se honā, v.n. To be in place, to be opportune or proper
jagah, adv. & postpn. In the place or room (of, -kī), instead (of).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جایگاه (jāygāh), جاگه (jâgah), from جا (jâ), جای (jây, “place”) + ـگاه (-gâh, “location suffix”). Compare Old Deccani جاگاہ (jāgāh). Cognate with Marathi जागा (jāgā), Bengali জায়গা (jaẏga), Assamese জেগা (zega).

ety: jagā˚ = Prk. जग्गाव(इ) or जग्गावे(इ); see jāgnā, of which it is the caus.
def: v.t. To waken, to rouse from sleep; to raise (the dead, or the wick of a lamp), to make bright or lively; to rouse, to stimulate
more: v. a. S. जागृ to wake. 1. To awake; rouse from sleep. 1. Saiyāṅ bhel nirmohiā, mori bārī niniā jagaīlak he . Mag. 2. Kachchī nīṅd meṅ jagānā achchhā nahīṅ . It is not good to wake one before he has had his quantum of sleep. 2. To raise the wick of a light. Dīvā jagā de . 3. To light; kindle. Jot jagā do . 3. To go over a faint writing or line. Harf jagānā. Lakīr jagānā. murdah jagānā , v. n. To wake the dead.

n. m. Lord of the universe; an incarnation of Vishnu. The great temple of this deity is situated on the coast of Orissa, and the great annual feast, called Rath-jātrā , is there held in the month of July, when the huge car of the idol is brought forth from the temple. 1. Apnā hāth Jagan-nāth . Prov. Your own right hand lord of the land. 2. Jagan Nāth kā bāṅṭā jis meṅ jhagṛā na jhāṅṭā . Prov. Each has his portion just at Jagan Nāth, No strife nor question as to this or that. Jagan Nāth kā bhāt , n. m. The rice partaken at Jagan Nāth by pilgrims of all castes out of the same pot; hence, something that all may eat of without losing caste.

def: s.m. The world, the universe; men and animals, created things; — a buttress; a curb of masonry round a well; — adj. Movable, locomotive, transitory
more: n. m. S. जगत from गम् to go. 1. The world. 1. Le-ke dīyā, kamā-ke khāyā, aisī taisī jagat meṅ āyā! (Rogues). What, take to give, and earning eat! To a hum-drum life like this submit! 2. Apnā maran jagat kī hāṅsī . Prov. What's death to one is food for laughter to the world. 3. Jo tū āyā yāi jagat meṅ, khā, pī, haṅs, bol, batā, Yā hi kāran sab ko ek hi ṭhaur sakelā hai, ye jag- darshan kā melā hai . Mauj. Since in this world you're come, eat, drink, laugh, speak, free commune share, For this together all are thrown, this world a pleasure fair. 4. Mohe jagat ne jān divānī! Āp haṅsī aur mohe rulānī! Wom. song. Ṭhumrī . The world doth call me mad! It laughs—to weep I'm made! 2. (n. f.) The rim of a well ( meṅḍ ). 1. Kuëṅ kī jagat ṭūḷī huī hai . 2. Gagrī to dhar āīṅ kueṅ kī jagat pe . Song. jagat-ātmā , n. m. The soul of the world, i. e. Brahmā . jagat-ujāgar , Enlightening the world. jagat-p…

ety: jāg˚ = Prk. जग्ग(इ) = S. जागर्(ति), rt. जागृ
def: v.n. To waken, be awake; to watch, keep vigil; to become vigilant, be on the alert; to be refreshed; to brighten
more: v. n. 1. To be awake; to watch; keep a vigil. 1. Jāge so pāve, sove so khove! Prov. Who wake get, who sleep lose. (The early bird picks the worm). 2. Jāgte rahīyo! Watchmen's cry. 3. Jāgyo, jāgnā bhalā hai . Mendicants' cry. Awake, to wake is best. 4. Chor se kahe tū chorī kar, sāh se kahe tū jāgtā rahīyo . Prov. The thief now bid to steal, the banker bid to keep a watch. (Said of double-dealing). 5. Jāg jagante paharvā, lāg lagante aur . The wakeful watchmen are, who union form with God quite different are. 6. Jāgnā hai, jāg le aflāk ke sāë tale; Hashr tak sotā rahegā khāk ke sāë tale . Wake if you are to wake beneath this grateful sky, Until the resurrection 'neath the earth you lie. 2. To be refreshed; to revive; flourish; brighten up; beam. 3. To come to one's senses; to be wide awake. Nasīb jāge Good fortune wakes. jāg paṛnā , v. n. To wake suddenly; to be roused ( chauṅk paṛnā ).
v. n. To awake, to wake, be awake. جاگ پڙنا jāg paṛnā, v. n. To awake.
subs: v.n. To waken, wake up, be roused; to brighten up, &c.

def: s.f. corr. of A. jaḥīm, q.v. the first letter of the Arabic, Persian, and Urdū alphabets. Its pronunciation depends on the vowel which accompanies it; e.g. ا with fatḥa (اَ) = the Devanāgarī अ = the English a, as in cedar (or = the English u in fun); ا with kasra (اِ) = the Devanāgarī इ = the English i, as in fin; ا with ẓamma (اُ) = the Devanāgarī उ = the English u, as in bull. — ا with fatḥa, followed by ا, (i.e. اَا, which is usually written آ) = the Devanāgarī आ = the English ā, as in father; ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اَو) = the Devanāgarī औ = the English au (sounded like ou in house); ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter ي, (i.e. اَي), = the Devanāgarī ऐ = the English ai, as in aisle. — ا with ẓamma, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اُو), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ऊ = the English u, as in rule (or = the oo in pool), and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ओ = the English o, as in pole. — ا with kasra, followed by ي, (i.e. اِي), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ई = the English ī, as in caprice; and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ए = the French e, as in fête. ا is used, — 1˚ (arithmetically, according to the Abjad, q.v.) for the number one. 2˚ (for अ, in Almanacks and Astrology) for Sunday, and Aries. 3˚ (for अ) as a name of the god Vishnu. 4˚ as an inseparable prefix to a word beginning with a consonant, to signify negation or privation: e.g. aśuddha, 'impure,' 'incorrect.' As in Greek, a becomes an before a vowel; e.g. ananta, 'without end,' 'endless.'

more: n. m. S. यजमान A client to whose custom Brahmans, barbers, washermen, and some others, have a prescriptive claim. (The hereditary Brahman, or barber, etc. of a village must be paid his fees, whether you choose to employ him or another person). Hansā the so uṛ gaë, aur kāgā bhaë divān; Jā, lamman, ghar āpne, singh kā ke jijmān? Dohā. The swan hath fled, the crow who fills his place is mad, Go, Brahman, home, from lions no one good hath had. ☞ The story is that the cupidity of a Brahman having led him to a lion's cave in hopes of bringing away the ornaments of the bodies he had devoured, was saved by his minister, the swan, who introduced the Brahman as the lion's spiritual preceptor. The new prime minister, the crow, however, was partial to carrion, and the lion generously warned the grasping Brahman against the sinister advice which the crow would be likely to give him.

s. m. A customer, a person to whose custom brāhmans, barbers, and some others, have a legal claim. (The hereditary brāhman, or barber, &c. of a village must be paid his fees, whether you choose to employ him or another person).

s.m. One who worships the more terrible attributes of the Deity; — a class of faqīrs, the followers of Saiyid Jalāl Buḵẖārī; — a kind of pigeon; — a fine species of wheat with reddish ears.
more: n. m. One who worships the more terrible attributes of Deity; a sect of Faqīrs; a follower of Jalāl-ud-dīn Bukhārī .
s. m. 1. A person who worships the more terrible attributes of the deity. 2. The followers of سيدجلال بخاري 3. A kind of pigeon.

def: s.m. corr. of janam or janma, q.v.
s. m. Flood, inundation.
adj. Ignitable, inflammable, combustible; passionate, fiery, choleric.
more: n. f. S. ज्वल to burn. 1. Burning; combustion; inflammation. 2. Burning; smart; throb. Phunsī meṅ abtak jalan hai . 3. Passion; rage; envy; jealousy; malice; hatred. Use to merī jalan hai. jalā-huā, jalā , adj. Set on fire; consumed; burned; scorched; charred; singed; scalded.
s. m. f. 1. Burning, heat, inflammation. 2. Passion, vexation.

ety: jalā˚ = Prk. जलाव (इ) or जलावे(इ) = S. ज्वल+आपय caus. augment
def: v.t. (caus. of jalnā), To burn; to light, kindle; to fire; to inflame; to excite envy or jealousy; to vex, to irritate, enrage, exasperate; to grieve, to cause anguish to, to consume with sorrow; to smart, pain
more: v. a. S. ज्वालन 1. To burn; light; kindle; fire. 2. To vex; provoke; enrage; incense; exasperate. Jalā jalā-kar mār utārā . Wom. I have vexed the life out of her. 3. To inflame; excite envy or jealousy; hurt the feelings. Phr.—To raise one's choler, work into a passion; make one's blood boil; lash into fury. 1. Jale ko jalāëṅge, non mirach lagāëṅge . Boys' rhyme. The burning we'll inflame, chillies and salt for game. 2. Jale ko jalāëṅge, halvā pūrī khāëṅge . Boys' rhyme. Let's burn afresh their burning hearts, before their eyes we'll eat our tarts. 3. Tan badan jalā-kar kīyā rākh charkhe ne, Dūsre jalānā līyā khasam ghar ke ne . Song. Lāonī . With toiling at the spinning wheel I am to ashes turned, Another torch the house-band doth apply, I am twice burned. jalāne-vālā , n. m. ( Med .) 1. An irritant. 2. Caustic.
( ज्वालन or caus. of جلنا) v. a. To burn, to kindle, to inflame, to fire, to light, to make jealous.
subs: adj. & s.m. (Med.) Irritant; caustic; — an irritant.

def: s.m. Dropsy, ascites; — flat alluvial land; name of a district in the Punjab (the soil of which is alluvial.)
Jalandhar (a city in Punjab, India)
s.m. Dropsy, ascites; — flat alluvial land; name of a district in the Punjab (the soil of which is alluvial.)
more: n. m. S. जलोदर 1. Dropsy. 2. The name of a district in the Panjab abounding in water.

def: adj. & s.m. Dropsical; — a dropsical person.
s. (dakh.) Burner.

ety: Prk. जलअप्पअं = S. ज्वल+क+त्व+कं; see jalnā
def: s.m. Heart-burning, heart-ache, grief, remorse; envy, jealousy; anger, rage, spite
more: Wom. n. m. Heart-burning; heartache; sorrow; grief. Saut kā ānā jīte jī kā jalāpā . Wom. A co-wife in the house is the burning of a living heart. dil-jalā , n. m. One smitten with love; a lover.

def: adj. (dakh.) Envious.
s. m. A deluge.
For this and other compounds (not given below) of which jal is the first member, see s.v. jal.

ety: rt. of jalā-nā+ū = Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Burning; combustible; irritable; irascible; furious; — a burner, &c.
more: adj. 1. Burning; combustible. 2. Furious; boiling over; infuriate. jalai'yā , n. m. lit . heart-burner. One beloved; a sweetheart. Mūṅh se kyoṅ nā bole, jalaiyā? Wom. song.

def: s. or adj. Burner, burning.
s.f. A horse-bridle; rein (of a bridle); retinue, equipage, train, suite, court; — splendour, pomp, state ( = jalwa)
subs: s.m. A court-yard, an area; a vestibule, porch, ante-chamber

def: s. f. A leech.
s.f. Fire-wood, fuel ( = jāran).

s.m. See s.v. jalā. s.m. A net; a web, spider's web, cobweb: a pellicle; a speck (in the eye), cataract; a large jar, an earthen vessel; mildew (on flour, &c.); a kind of water-weed (used in refining sugar)
more: adj. Banished. jalā-vatan k., des nikālā denā , v. a. To banish from one's country.
subs: v.n. To be overgrown with weeds.

def: s.f. corr. of next, q.v.
n. f. Banishment; exile.
part. adj. (f. jalāʼī), Kindled, ignited; inflamed.
(from جل and بـﮩنا q. v.) s. f. Swimming, striking with the arms and legs in water.
part. adj. = jalā-balā, q.v.s.v. jalā; — see also next.
more: part. Burnt up or consumed with fire. See جلابلا.

def: adv. Vexed, annoyed, in anger, angrily, in a rage, &c. (see jalnā).
adj. Aquatick (as birds, but not fish), moving or going in water.
adv. Swift, quick, fleet; expeditious, active, nimble, brisk; hasty, impetuous, rash, precipitate; fierce
more: adv. Without loss of time; quickly. jald-bāz , adj. 1. Quick; swift; fleet; expeditious. 2. Hasty; precipitate.

adj. Expeditious, quick, nimble, brisk, fast, hasty, impetuous, rash, fierce; quickly. جلدباز jald-bāz, adj. Expeditious, fleet, hasty. جلدبازي jald-baz-ī, s. f. Haste, expedition, agility. جلدقدم jald-ḳadam, adj. Fleet. جلدمزاج jald-mizāj, adj. Passionate, hasty.
subs: adj. & s.m. Quick, &c. = jald; — an active person; a hasty or impetuous man
s.f. Quickness, briskness, activity, agility; haste, expedition; precipitation, &c. ( = jaldī)

def: network
quick; quickly; fast
s.f. Quickness, haste, speed, swiftness, celerity; expedition, despatch; hurry, precipitation, rashness, abruptness, impetuosity; adv. Quickly, &c. (see what follows)
more: n. f. 1. Quickness; rapidity; swiftness; celerity; expedition; despatch. 2. Haste; hurry; precipitation; abruptness; impetuosity. jaldī se qabzā k . v. a. To lose no time in taking possession. jaldī k . v. a. 1. To make haste; to hasten; hurry. 2. To be precipitate.
s. f. Quickness, haste, hurry, celerity, precipitation, rashness, abruptness, impetuosity. adv. Quickly. جلدي کرني jaldī karnī, To make haste or hasten, to hurry, to be precipitate or rash.
subs: adv. Quickly, with all speed, soon, without delay ( = jald)
v.n. To make haste, to hasten, hurry; to be rash or precipitate.
ex: جلدی کیجیے!
jaldī kījīye!
make haste, hurry up

subs: s.m. The goglet in which water is cooled with nitre.

more: E. n. f. A kind of sweetmeat. Jalebiyoṅ kī rakhvālī aur choṭṭī kutyā! Prov. The thief of a dog set to watch o'er the sweets! jale'bā , W. n. m. Mulberry ( shahtūt ). Kāṭ kī lakṛī kā banā hai jalebā! Delhi street cry. Buy my jaleba (sweetmeat) out of a woody stem! peṭ meṅ jalebī honā , Tir. v. n. To plot or meditate evil designs in secret. jaṅgal-jalebī , n. f. Excrement; filth.
s. f. A kind of sweetmeat.
s. f. Belonging to the retinue.

subs: s.m. The goglet in which water is cooled with nitre.

ety: Prk. जालिआ; S. जालिका; see jāl, and jālā
def: s.f. Network; a net (for holding grass, straw, &c.); — an ox-muzzle; a lattice; a grating; trellis-work; lace, bobbinet; the integument in which a fœtus is enveloped; a coil; a caul; the pellicle or coating of a fruit-seed (as that of the mango).
more: n. f. 1. Network; lattice-work. 2. Bobbinnet. 3. A net for holding grass or straw in. Ek ko de hai rutba-i-ālī, Ek ko de hai khurpa o jālī . This one on high in place of honor set, To that one given a sickle and grass not. 4. The integument in which the fœtus is inclosed; a childs' caul. 5. The thick coating of a mango stone. Abhī ām meṅ jālī nahīṅ paṛī .
s. f. 1. Network. 2. The integument in which a fœtus is enveloped. 3. A coil, a caul. 4. Lattice, grate, trellis-work. جالي پڙنا jālī paṛnā, expresses the hardening of the stone of a mango.
s. f. (dakh.) Lace, edging ( Harris ). جالي موي بند jālī-mūʼe-band (dakh.) A garland like network tied to the forehead, and covering the forepart of the head, worn only by women ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: adj. Clear, manifest, apparent, evident, conspicuous; large, plain (hand-writing); distinctly-sounded (as a letter)
more: adj. Conspicuous; plain; large and plain (handwriting). jalī qalam ke harf . Capital letters. khat-i-jalī , n. m. Large, plain handwriting. fasl-i-jalī , E. n. f. The season before the commencement of the rains in which a crop of rice is cut.
adj. 1. Plain, apparent. 2. Large, plain (hand writing). فصل جلي faṣl-i-jalī, A season before the commencement of the rains in which a crop of rice is cut.
subs: s.f. The season before the commencement of the rains, in which a crop of rice is cut.

def: adj. Forged, fabricated, counterfeit, fictitious, artificial; spurious, false, supposititious (as a child).
more: adj. 1. Forged; fabricated; fictitious; counterfeit. 2. Spurious; not genuine; false; vicious. jālī beṭā ; H. banāoṭī beṭā , n. m. Supposititious child. jālī dastāvez banānā , v. n. To forge a document. jālī kāgaz . A forged paper or document.

(from the Greek), prop.n.m. The physician Galen.

s.m.f. A companion, comrade, chum.

s.m. A prompt, ready, or eloquent tongue.

n. m. lit . destroyed by water. Dried up, parched (soil, etc.). jal'lā , n. m. A lake ( jhīl ); reservoir. jal-mandar , n. m. A summer house in the midst of water; subterraneous apartments constructed on the bank of a river. jal-nīm, patāl-nīm , n. m. A bitter herb which grows on the banks of ponds, etc.

adj. Great, glorious, eminent
more: v. 3. pers. past sing. (from جلال) Is eminent in majesty or glory. جل جلاله jalla jalālu-hu or جل شانه jalla shānu-hu, Eminent is His (Godʼs) glory or majesty.
subs: jalla shānu-hu, Eminent is His glory or majesty

def: s.m. (orig.) One whose office it is to flog others with a whip; (in the present day) an executioner; — adj. Cruel, merciless, hard-hearted
more: n. m. جلد he hurt his skin. One who skins or flays; hence; an executioner. Martā hūṅ is āwāz pe harchand sir uṛ /?/āë, Jallād ko lekin woh kahe jāën ki 'hāṅ aur .' Gālib. I would my life might in that voice but melt away, 'Strike,' headsman, 'strike again,' would she but constant say. jallād-i-falak, mirrīkh ; H. maṅgal , n. m. (Ast). Mars.
s. m. An executioner; (and, hence) cruel, hardhearted (q. d. one who skins or flays, from جلد The skin). جلاد فلک jallād-i-falak, The planet Mars.

def: s.f. The office of an executioner; cruelty, villainy.
more: s. f. The office of an executioner, cruelty, villainy.

def: adj. & s.m. Cruel, harsh, severe; inconsiderate, rash, acting without thinking; vile, contemptible; — a despised or contemptible person, a low person, one of a degraded tribe or occupation; a rogue; a wretch, a miscreant.
s. m. A waterman, a mermaid.
s.f. Darkness, &c.
v.t. (prov.). See jārnā and jalānā.
